name: Lumpia (Philippine Egg Rolls)
recipeIngredient: Raw shrimp: ½ pound, chopped very fine
Scallions: 4 large, up to 6, chopped (white and green parts)
Water chestnuts: 4 ounces (from 1 small can), chopped fine
Soy sauce: ¼ cup, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together all ingredients except egg roll wrappers and oil with a spoon, keeping the mixture as uncooked as possible. Taste the filling in a small skillet or microwave and adjust seasoning by adding more pepper, soy sauce, and Accent if desired. Chill the filling for 1 hour or until firm.
2. Cut each wrapper in half lengthwise. Heat corn oil to a depth of 3 inches, or until a dropped bread cube sizzles instantly (about 360 degrees F with a thermometer).
3. Spread about 1 tablespoon of filling evenly along the long top edge of each halved wrapper, leaving a half-inch border.
4. Fold the border over the line of filling and lightly mark it with water using your forefinger.
5. Roll up the wrapper tightly, sealing it with another line of water applied with your finger. Water acts as a sealant to prevent unwrapping.
6. Cut each egg roll into thirds using a sharp knife. Fry them in batches in hot oil until golden brown and crispy, turning often. Drain on paper towels and serve immediately.
7. The sauce is optional. Mix equal parts soy sauce and white vinegar with minced fresh ginger and scallions to taste, in addition to the two sauces listed above.
Makes 120 tiny egg rolls.
